issues.description: harden_converge_clean does not persist PR URL before attempting merge. After `record.spec_pr_url = Some(pr_url)` (line 1227), the code proceeds directly to `merge_pr` (line 1231) without calling `self.store.update_loop(record).await?`. In contrast, review_converge_clean correctly persists at line 1278. If auto_merge is enabled and merge_pr fails (network error, merge conflict), the PR URL is lost in memory. On the next tick, the idempotency guard (`spec_pr_url.is_none()`) passes again and a duplicate PR is created.
issues.suggestion: Add `self.store.update_loop(record).await?;` after setting `record.spec_pr_url = Some(pr_url)` at line 1227, before the auto_merge block, mirroring the pattern in review_converge_clean at line 1278.

issues.description: Review Continue-at-max-rounds discards judge reasoning in failure message. When the judge returns Continue at max_rounds (line 1137), failure_reason is set to generic 'Max implement rounds (N) exceeded' without the judge's reasoning. The equivalent harden path (line 708) correctly includes it. This was flagged in review rounds 5 and 7 and remains unfixed. The judge's analysis of why it wanted to continue is valuable diagnostic info for the engineer.
issues.suggestion: Change the failure_reason at line 1140 to: `format!("Max implement rounds ({}) exceeded. Judge wanted to continue: {}", record.max_rounds, judge_output.reasoning)`

issues.description: NFR-5 requires integration tests in control-plane/tests/judge_integration.rs that wire a mock judge into the full driver tick and assert correct state transitions plus judge_decisions rows. No such file exists. The spec lists this as a specific acceptance requirement: 'full driver tick with mock judge returning each decision variant; assert correct state transitions and judge_decisions rows written.' Unit tests in judge.rs test the judge module in isolation but do not exercise the driver integration.
issues.suggestion: Create control-plane/tests/judge_integration.rs with tests that construct a ConvergentLoopDriver with a mock judge, execute ticks through both review and harden paths with each JudgeDecision variant, and assert resulting LoopState and judge_decisions records in the store.

issues.description: FR-1b spec deviation at round 2 with high-severity findings: determine_trigger returns None for round < 3 when findings include high/critical severity and there are no recurring findings. FR-1b states verdict.clean == false is a trigger. This means the judge cannot override-accept a round-2 verdict where the reviewer over-classified severity — partially defeating Problem 1 (triviality override). The code has a detailed comment explaining the tradeoff, but the spec deviation is not documented in any external doc or config option for operators to control.
issues.suggestion: Add a configurable `judge_min_round` option to OrchestratorConfig (defaulting to 3) so operators can control the early-round skip threshold. Alternatively, add this to the spec as an acknowledged deviation.

issues.description: Review path: when judge returns ExitClean via invoke_judge_for_stage, the call chain is invoke_judge_for_stage -> judge.evaluate -> ... -> persist decision with record.round. Then review_converge_clean is called. But invoke_judge_for_stage takes `record: &LoopRecord` (immutable ref), yet the judge needs the round for persistence. Looking at the caller, record is `&mut LoopRecord` but passed as `&LoopRecord` to invoke_judge_for_stage. The round used for persistence inside evaluate() is correct (pre-increment). However, in the Continue path, `record.round += 1` happens at line 1158 AFTER the judge persistence — this is correct. No actual bug here, but the immutable borrow pattern means the judge cannot accidentally mutate the record.
issues.suggestion: No code change needed — noting this is correctly structured.

issues.description: FR-7a TOCTOU window between judge_decision_stats query and create_judge_decision. The migration adds a partial unique index `idx_judge_decisions_one_exit_clean_per_loop` as a DB-level safety net, which is good. However, the MemoryStateStore has no equivalent constraint — the write lock is not held between the stats read and the decision write. In tests using MemoryStateStore with concurrent access (if any), the one-shot guard could fail. For single-instance Stage 1 this is acceptable, but MemoryStateStore should enforce the same invariant for test fidelity.
issues.suggestion: Add a check in MemoryStateStore::create_judge_decision that rejects a second exit_clean for the same loop_id, mirroring the DB constraint. This ensures tests faithfully simulate production behavior.

issues.description: Harden audit-found-issues path: when the judge fallback fires (disabled/error/timeout) at the else branch (line 726), there's a max_rounds check before dispatch_revise. But in the judge Continue path (line 688), there's also a max_rounds guard. These two paths handle max_rounds differently: the judge path includes reasoning in the failure message, the fallback path uses a generic message. This is correct behavior but creates an asymmetry in user-facing error messages between judge-enabled and judge-disabled modes at max_rounds.
issues.suggestion: Minor: align error message format between the two paths for consistency. No functional issue.

issues.description: compute_recurring_findings deserializes issues from every prior round's output JSON on every evaluate() call. With the 10-call ceiling, this means up to 10 full round-history deserializations per loop. Each deserialization creates Issue structs that are immediately dropped after comparison. For loops with many rounds and issues, this is O(N*M) work repeated on every judge call.
issues.suggestion: Acceptable for Stage 1 given the 10-call ceiling. Consider caching the deserialized prior issues in the JudgeInput computation or passing them from the caller.

issues.description: JudgeDecision has both serde Deserialize and a manual as_str() method that must stay in sync. Adding a new variant requires updating both. The as_str() method is used for logging and persistence, while serde is used for JSON parsing. A test verifies roundtrip (test_judge_decision_serde_roundtrip), which is good.
issues.suggestion: No change needed — the roundtrip test catches drift. Consider using strum or a derive macro in the future to auto-generate as_str().

issues.description: FR-6c asks for judge decisions inline with each round. The implementation nests them correctly in RoundSummary and renders them in the CLI. The flat judge_decisions array in InspectResponse is useful for API consumers but is not rendered in the CLI, which is fine. Inline display satisfies the spec.
